title: zieria veronicea subsp. insularis
intro: Zieria veronicea subsp. insularis, also known as Lord Howe Island zieria, is a plant species endemic to the Lord Howe Island in Australia, belonging to the family Rutaceae and the genus Zieria. The plant has been traditionally used by the indigenous people for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which contain essential oils with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Zieria veronicea subsp. insularis include its potential to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, tincture, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
